details.text: Derivative: الْكَافِرُونَ is derived from the word كَفَرَ – كُفْر. It is in the اِسْمُ الفَاعِل (Ism ul-Faa’il – Active participle) form which implies those who are engaged in كُفْر (Kufr - disbelief) and continue to be engaged in it. It differs from the use of the verb as it implies continuity i.e., disbelievers who are firmly attached to and entrenched in their disbelief. كُفْر (Kufr) refers to disbelief, rejection and refusal. كُفْر (Kufr) in the Arabic language is also used to refer to the denial of a favour by being ungrateful for it.
- يَا أَيُّهَا is used in formal settings and is an address, announcement and call to a serious or significant matter.

Derivative: أَعْبُدُ and تَعْبُدُونَ come from عَبَدَ – عِبَادَة which comprises the meanings of both worship and being enslaved. Ibn Taymiyah (rh) stated there are five components to عِبَادَة (‘Ibaadah) or being an عَبْد (‘Abd - slave) to Allah which can be summarised as follows: 1) To have obedience to Allah above obedience to any other thing or matter. 2) To have intense love for Allah above love for anyone or anything else. 3) To have Tawakkul (trust and reliance) upon Allah. 4) Sincerity in that the act is done purely for Allah’s sake. 5) The recognition of the terms of slavery where these terms are decided and dictated by Allah alone i.e., man does not worship Allah according to the way that he choses or wishes but rather in the manner that has been dictated by Allah.

لَا أَعْبُدُ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ

- “I do not worship (لَا أَعْبُدُ) what (مَا) you (plural) worship (تَعْبُدُونَ)” – It implies that I do not worship what you worship in terms of idols and I do not enslave myself to what you enslave yourselves to in terms of desires.

Derivative: عَابِدُونَ is a noun of the اِسْمُ الفَاعِل (Ism ul-Faa’il) form which implies continuity and permanence. It carries the implication that you have not been worshipping, you are not worshipping and you will not be worshipping in the future.

وَلَا أَنتُمْ عَابِدُونَ مَا أَعْبُدُ

- The مَا (Maa) here in the statement مَا أَعْبُدُ does not mean “what” I worship. Rather, the مَا is مَا المَصْدَرِية (Maa Al-Masdariyah) with the implied meaning of “the kind or form of ‘Ibaadah (عِبادَة) that I engage in” i.e., the meaning of the verse is: “The worship that I do, you will never do”.
- مَا (Maa) can also be for purpose of تَعَجُّب (Ta’ajjub – To express wonder). When مَن (Man – who) is used it is to identify an identity but when مَا (Maa) is used it is for identifying qualities. The meaning that is implied is therefore: “You will never worship the amazing and true Allah that I worship”.

Derivative: دِينِ (Deen) comes from the word دَانَ – يَدِينُ – دَيْن (Dain). دَيْن (Dain) means a loan and a debt that is owed. It can refer to reward and recompense like in the statement يَوْمُ الدِّين “The day of recompense” where everyone will be repaid what they are due. دِينِ (Deen) can also be used to refer to “judgement” in addition to all that the message of Islam encompasses in terms of beliefs and rulings, like in His statement: دِينُ الحَقَّ “The Deen of truth”.

لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ وَلِيَ دِينِ

- “Your Deen is (only) for you and my Deen is (only) for me” implying that your Deen will never go beyond you to others in terms of myself and the believers and my Deen is only for me i.e., I will never be leaving it. This verse is a clear statement concluding the previous statements in this Surah to make clear that there will be no shifting of positions or compromises from the Prophet ﷺ and the believers in spite of the efforts of the disbelievers.
